body:
## Summary

The Claude review lane can fail during session setup when the self-hosted runner's Claude Code settings use `permissions.defaultMode: "auto"` while the repo runtime installs `acpx@^0.4.0`.

Observed run/job:
- https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/actions/runs/25175391392/job/73805965114

## Symptoms

The job logs show the `Run claude review` step invoking `acpx`, then failing before any agent output is produced:

```text
{"msg":"acpx completed","exit_code":1,"session_ensure_outcome":"failed"}
{"msg":"acpx run failed","stderr_tail":"session setup failed: Internal error\n"}
##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.
```

There is also a non-fatal warning earlier about failing to push thread state with `github-actions[bot]`, but the direct job failure is the `acpx` session setup error.

## Reproduction / diagnosis

On the same runner configuration, `acpx@0.4.0` fails to create a Claude session when `~/.claude/settings.json` contains:

```json
"permissions": {
  "allow": [],
  "defaultMode": "auto"
}
```

The more detailed local repro error is:

```text
Error handling request ... method: 'session/new' ...
{
  code: -32603,
  message: 'Internal error',
  data: { details: 'Invalid permissions.defaultMode: auto.' }
}
Internal error
```

Testing with `acpx@0.6.1` successfully creates the Claude session with the same Claude setting, so this appears to be an older ACP adapter/runtime compatibility issue.

## Suggested fix

Update the agent runtime dependency from `acpx@^0.4.0` to a version that supports current Claude Code settings, e.g. `acpx@^0.6.1`, and refresh the lockfile.

Short-term workaround: change/remove `permissions.defaultMode: "auto"` in the runner's `~/.claude/settings.json`, but that is runner-local and brittle.

comments.body:
Additional check:

- Current Claude Code reports `--permission-mode` choices as: `acceptEdits`, `auto`, `bypassPermissions`, `default`, `dontAsk`, `plan`.
- So `permissions.defaultMode: "auto"` is the correct mode name for current Claude Code; this is not a settings typo.
- Local repro with the same `~/.claude/settings.json`:
  - `acpx@0.4.0` fails with `Invalid permissions.defaultMode: auto.`
  - `acpx@0.4.1` succeeds (`@agentclientprotocol/claude-agent-acp@^0.25.0`)
  - `acpx@0.5.0`, `0.5.3`, and `0.6.1` also succeed.

Because the workflow uses `npm ci`, the lockfile can still pin `acpx@0.4.0` even though package.json has `^0.4.0`; the fix should update package-lock as well. Runner Node is already 22.21.1, which satisfies newer acpx's `>=22.12.0` engine requirement.
Related but separate finding: the Codex review lane is also unhealthy, but it hangs after `Running acpx` rather than failing Claude session setup. Tracked separately in #70.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777569497870 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzkyMzk1MDI6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6Njk6aW1wbGVtZW50OjY5OjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-69/codex-25179270284`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/71

Updated the agent runtime dependency from `acpx@^0.4.0` to `acpx@^0.6.1` and refreshed `.agent/package-lock.json` so workflow `npm ci` installs `acpx@0.6.1`. Verified the lockfile resolution with `npm ls acpx --package-lock-only` and ran `.agent` `npm test` successfully.

title: Claude review lane fails with permissions.defaultMode auto on acpx 0.4.0
